title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection · Transfer Build
summary: An independent accessibility club receives a different grid, seed, algorithm, input profile, display scale, assistance need, platform, and failure condition and transfers correctness, completeness, optimality, metrics, and selection into a changed solver benchmark and decision report. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of solver benchmark and decision report with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.
State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Correctness Witness
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab puzzle team handles case 7592 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate endpoints, adjacency, passability, uniqueness policy, and reported cost against an independent route checker.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Solver Selection
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-solver-selection-103fv6" that uses case 7073 to compare solver correctness, completeness, optimality, work, memory, and selection on identical fixtures. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Solver Selection.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Solver Selection into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Solver Selection, keep Solver Selection connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-solver-selection-103fv6
# Competency: maze-solver-selection.
# Case 7073: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_solver_selection_103fv6(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_103fv6 = "103fv6-6vf301-7073"
    assert evidence_variant_103fv6
    evidence_axes_103fv6 = (
        "grid103fv6",
        "topology7073",
        "search6vf301",
        "event103fv67073",
        "access7073103fv6",
        "recovery6vf3017073",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_103fv6)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"
